reasoning:
Verdict: REJECT (10% confidence)

Module Scores:
  Sentinel (CWE/OWASP Web Application Security): 50/100 [Low risk] — 1 info
  Watchdog (OWASP LLM Top 10 / Cisco AI Threat Taxonomy): 99/100 [Info risk] — 1 info
  Guardian (NIST AI RMF / EU AI Act / UNICC Responsible AI): 15/100 [Critical risk] — 4 critical, 6 high, 4 medium

Arbitration Triggers:
  • REJECT trigger — score below 30: Guardian (15/100)
  • REJECT trigger — critical findings in Guardian: [guardian-1] No governance documentation; README.md missing; [guardian-2] No model cards for ResNet and BERT components; model provenance undocumented; [guardian-3] No documented bias testing or fairness evaluation for demographic-impacting AI; [guardian-4] Autonomous content moderation with minimal human oversight; no kill switches or escalation workflows

Disagreement Resolution:
  ⚠ Watchdog scored 99/100 (info risk) while Sentinel scored 50/100 (low risk) — Δ49 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Sentinel to maintain safety margins.
  ⚠ Sentinel scored 50/100 (low risk) while Guardian scored 15/100 (critical risk) — Δ35 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Guardian to maintain safety margins.
  ⚠ Watchdog scored 99/100 (info risk) while Guardian scored 15/100 (critical risk) — Δ84 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Guardian to maintain safety margins.

Confidence Factors:
  • Base: 1/3 module(s) independently support REJECT — 30% baseline conviction
  • -30% confidence: 3 disagreement(s) between modules
  • Average module score across 3 completed module(s): 55/100 (reported for context — not directly used in confidence calibration)

--- LLM-Enhanced Council Narrative ---
Overall, the three assessments consistently identify a foundational problem: absence of source materials blocks a confident technical review (sentinel-1-6be65112, watchdog-1), and multiple modules call out secrets/credential and data governance shortcomings (sentinel recommendations; watchdog secrets guidance; guardian-10 on DB controls). These agreements underscore that discovery (code, infra, keys) and basic security hygiene must come first before deeper audits.
However, there are substantive conflicts in risk framing and scope. Sentinel's technical rating is relatively modest while guardian deems the product critically risky on governance, compliance, and human‑oversight grounds (guardian-4, guardian-1, guardian-8). Watchdog narrows LLM-specific risk (watchdog-1) and therefore deprioritises LLM guardrails, which conflicts with guardian's high‑risk classification that applies regardless of whether an LLM is present. Finally, notable gaps remain unaddressed by any single module: adversarial robustness for CV/NLP pipelines, operational monitoring and drift detection, and end‑to‑end ML supply‑chain assurances (SBOMs, signed images, CI/CD gating). Addressing these discovery, governance, security hygiene, and operational gaps should be the immediate next step before conciliating the differing risk severity judgements.

top_findings: Insufficient source code provided for security analysis
sentinel.summary: VeriMedia AI is described as a Flask-based AI-powered media verification platform with deep learning, NLP, social media integration, and autonomous content moderation capabilities. However, no source code was provided for analysis, making a complete security assessment impossible.
sentinel.recommendation: Submit the full VeriMedia AI source code repository to enable comprehensive security analysis. Once code is available, priority areas will include: (1) authentication and authorization on all REST API endpoints, (2) input validation and injection prevention for user-uploaded content, (3) secrets and credential management in configuration and deployment, and (4) dependency vulnerability scanning.

top_findings: No LLM/LLM-integration code detected in provided source materials (ResNet/BERT-only ML stack referenced)
watchdog.summary: VeriMedia AI — profile indicates an ML stack (ResNet for CV, BERT for NLP) but no LLM integrations (GPT/Claude/Llama/etc.) were present in the supplied materials. No source code was provided for verification, so no concrete LLM-related code paths could be audited.
watchdog.recommendation:
Top 3 priority remediation actions:
1) Inventory and scan all code, containers, and infra for LLM usage: search for 'openai', 'anthropic', 'cohere', 'langchain', 'llamaindex', 'huggingface', 'Pinecone/Weaviate/Milvus', and environment variables like OPENAI_API_KEY. If found, treat those services as in-scope and run the checks below.  
2) If any LLM endpoints are present, immediately implement guardrails: prevent prompt injection by using structured prompts/typed inputs, do not concatenate raw us

top_findings: No model cards for ResNet and BERT components; model provenance undocumented
No documented bias testing or fairness evaluation for demographic-impacting AI
guardian.summary: VeriMedia AI is a high-risk autonomous content moderation platform that lacks foundational governance, documentation, and compliance infrastructure. The application performs autonomous authenticity verification and misinformation flagging affecting user rights to expression and information access—triggering EU AI Act high-risk classification—yet provides zero model cards, no documented bias testing, no human oversight workflows, and no privacy or accountability mechanisms. Create docs/oversight-policy.md and implement in src/moderation.py: route all moderation decisions with confidence <0.95 to human review, establish 24-hour SLA, document kill switch procedure to halt autonomous decisions if false positive rate exceeds 5%.
